note: Reviewer subagents were not callable from the agent context (no Task/SendMessage tool surfaced). The producing agent performed a structured self-review against the security-auditor methodology. This report should be re-run by the team lead with the actual dev-security-auditor subagent before merging.

owasp_coverage.A01_broken_access_control: n/a — code only reads procfs as the running user; no privilege boundaries crossed.
owasp_coverage.A03_injection: Path injection: pid is an int and formatted via fmt.Sprintf(%d). filepath.Clean applied before os.Open. No user-controlled strings reach the filesystem.
owasp_coverage.A04_insecure_design: Design surfaces failure modes explicitly: missing file, malformed line, missing IO keys all return wrapped errors.
owasp_coverage.A05_security_misconfiguration: CheckIOAvailable surfaces EACCES at startup so the caller can disable IO columns gracefully.
owasp_coverage.A06_vulnerable_components: No new dependencies introduced; only stdlib imports used.
owasp_coverage.A08_data_integrity: Parsers validate suffix length before indexing (no out-of-bounds risk). ParseFloat errors are surfaced rather than silently zeroed.
owasp_coverage.A09_logging_failures: No logging in this layer (consistent with sibling parsers).

secret_scan: No hardcoded secrets, tokens, or credentials.
